skill.name: defi-expert
skill.description: Review DeFi protocol integrations for token decimals, denominations, addresses, and API data formats before implementation.
skill.summary: Checks DeFi integration details that often cause protocol errors or value loss.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and dismissed as false positives. SKILL.md contains instructional DeFi guidance only, with no executable commands, network access, credential access, or prompt injection text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Detected as External Command
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 59 through 68 contain a fenced TypeScript example showing correct token unit handling. The backticks are Markdown formatting, not executable Ruby or shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected text is a Markdown code fence around documentation. No skill script or runtime command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 3 describes DeFi protocol review triggers. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain skill metadata and contains no cryptographic algorithm use. The static label is not supported by the file context.

content.value_statement: DeFi integrations often fail because token units, decimals, and protocol formats are easy to confuse. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused checks before code is written or reviewed.
actual_capabilities: Checks token decimals for assets such as USDC, USDT, WBTC, ETH, and DAI.
Reviews denomination choices such as wei, ray, wad, and basis points.
Prompts validation of checksummed addresses before integration work.
Highlights common precision mistakes in swaps, liquidations, and flash loans.
Supports reviews for Aave, Compound, Uniswap, Curve, and Balancer integrations.
limitations: Does not execute code, query blockchains, or verify live protocol state.
Does not replace formal smart contract audits or protocol documentation.
Requires current token and protocol details from the user or project context.
Provides guidance, not financial, legal, or investment advice.

use_cases.title: Validate Integration Inputs
use_cases.description: Check token decimals, units, and addresses before implementing a protocol adapter.

use_cases.title: Review DeFi Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Find precision and denomination mistakes before a DeFi change is merged.

use_cases.title: Plan Protocol Support
use_cases.description: Create a checklist for supporting Aave, Compound, Uniswap, Curve, or Balancer.

prompt_templates.title: Check Token Units
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this DeFi task for token decimal and denomination risks before implementation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check before writing integration code.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Protocol Adapter
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this protocol adapter plan for address validation, token decimals, and protocol-specific units.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Precision Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this DeFi code path for precision loss, incorrect scaling, and unsafe amount conversions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewer checking swap, liquidation, or flash loan logic.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Protocol Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a protocol integration checklist for these DeFi protocols and identify unit, API, and data format risks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning across multiple DeFi protocols.

output_examples.input: Review a USDC payment integration for unit handling.
output_examples.output: Flags that USDC commonly uses 6 decimals, recommends reading decimals from the token contract, and checks parseUnits usage.
Notes that Aave rates may use ray precision and asks whether raw values need 27-decimal scaling.

output_examples.input: Create a checklist for a Uniswap swap integration.
output_examples.output: Covers token decimals, slippage basis points, allowance checks, checksummed addresses, and output amount validation.

best_practices: Provide the target protocol, token list, and expected units with each request.
Verify decimals from token contracts or trusted protocol documentation.
Ask for a checklist before writing code that moves or prices assets.
anti_patterns: Do not assume every ERC-20 token uses 18 decimals.
Do not mix wei, wad, ray, and basis points without explicit conversion.
Do not treat this skill as a replacement for live protocol verification.

faq.question: Which protocols does this skill mention directly?
faq.answer: It mentions Aave, Compound, Uniswap, Curve, and Balancer.

faq.question: Can it verify live token decimals on chain?
faq.answer: No. It can remind the assistant to verify decimals, but it does not query blockchains.

faq.question: Does it write complete DeFi integrations?
faq.answer: It guides review and planning. Implementation still depends on the surrounding project and libraries.

faq.question: Why does it focus on USDC and WBTC?
faq.answer: These tokens often break assumptions because USDC uses 6 decimals and WBTC uses 8 decimals.

faq.question: Is this a financial advice skill?
faq.answer: No. It addresses technical integration risks, not investment decisions.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only false positives in Markdown documentation.
